mnemonics: Regression: Rats Go Eating Granola Constantly
Multiple Linear Regression: Many Lines Increase Naturally Together
Polynomial Regression: Perfectly Lazy Elephants Navigate Mountains Justly
Logistic Regression: Little Ones Grow Into Great Solvers Transcending Mathematics Justly
keyPoints: Regression analysis is used to establish a relationship between two or more variables.
Linear Regression is a type of regression analysis that assumes a linear relationship between the independent and dependent variables.
Multiple Linear Regression is an extension of Linear Regression that allows for more than one independent variable.
Polynomial Regression is a type of regression analysis that assumes a polynomial relationship between the independent and dependent variables.
Logistic Regression is a type of regression analysis used for binary classification problems.
